purpose: Pre-specialist-dispatch coverage verification for v0 cold-start lane (rev3 proposal).

games_by_year.note: September only per Odds API archive boundary

odds_storage_pattern_finding.issue: Historical Odds API backfill stored snapshotted_at as the API call wall-clock time (~02:55:38 UTC of the day after game date), not the actual snapshot wall-clock time. For night games (most MLB games, 7-10pm ET = 23-02 UTC), the recorded snap timestamp lands AFTER the game's first pitch. For day games (afternoon ET), the recorded snap timestamp lands BEFORE the game.
odds_storage_pattern_finding.evidence: scripts/backfill-historical-odds/run.ts line 112-117: snapshotParam() returns nextDay 03:00 UTC of game_date. All 8,858 closing-snapshot rows in 2022-09 to 2024 carry exactly this single-timestamp-per-fetch-batch pattern. Distinct snap timestamps in 2024: 182 (one per game-date), 4051 rows total.
odds_storage_pattern_finding.impact_on_T_minus_60_pin: A literal `snapshotted_at <= game_time_utc - interval '60 minutes'` filter excludes ~37% of rows (the night-game-stamped-after-game ones), leaving 1,975 / 5,339 finals = 37% coverage.
odds_storage_pattern_finding.documented_workaround_in_spec: docs/features/moneyline-v0-feature-spec.md Coordination Note #6: 'If only a single closing line is available for historical games, market_log_odds_home in training uses the closing line (best available proxy for T-60) with this assumption documented in architecture.md.'
odds_storage_pattern_finding.interpretive_question: The proxy interpretation requires the audit gate to use `<= game_time_utc` (strict pregame) instead of `<= as_of`. This conflicts with the look-ahead audit script's hardcoded `<= :as_of` filter requirement. Resolution requires either (a) re-pulling Odds API with correct snapshot timestamps, or (b) accepting the proxy with audit script tuned to `<= game_time_utc` for the anchor only.

pitcher_game_log_status: PASS (100% game coverage on training window)
batter_game_log_status: FAIL (table is empty; 0 rows). Backfill script scripts/backfill-db/08-batter-game-log.mjs exists but has not been executed. Required for features 8 and 9 (team_wrcplus_l30_home/away).
weather_status: PASS (>93% coverage on all components; meteostat backfill landed)
park_factor_runs_status: PASS (30 venues, all with bearing; 8 domes flagged)
probable_starters_status: PASS (>99% coverage; spec allows games.probable_*_pitcher_id fallback for game_starters)
lineup_entries_status: Not separately verified; v0 spec uses roster fallback when lock check returns false. Roster fallback path is the dominant code path at T-60 anyway.

blockers: Odds T-60 pin coverage well below the brief's 3,500-row floor (1,975 games pre-drop, ~1,790 post-warmup; 51% of floor).
batter_game_log is empty; team_wrcplus features (8 and 9) cannot be built until backfill runs.

Snapshot timestamp storage in historical odds backfill records API call time, not snapshot wall-clock time. This is an interpretive question between strict-pin enforcement and the spec's Coordination Note #6 proxy allowance.
non_blockers: Weather, park factors, probable starters all clear coverage thresholds.
summary.specialist_dispatch_decision: HALT - escalate to user before dispatching mlb-data-engineer / mlb-feature-eng. The sample-size shortfall is large enough (37% of brief floor) that the proposal's primary assumption (n=200-400 graded picks at +2% EV) is at risk.

options_for_user.option: A: Run batter backfill, accept proxy anchor, ship with reduced sample
actions: Apply Coordination Note #6 proxy interpretation: use `<= game_time_utc` strict-pregame filter for the anchor only, document in architecture.md
Proceed with 1,790 train games / projected 90-180 graded picks at +2% EV
Tighten to +3% EV if needed per proposal's mitigation clause; bootstrap CIs handle variance
Sub-300-sample rule applies: lower CI bound on ROI AND CLV >= -1% to ship
options_for_user.risk: Empirical bar may not clear at this sample size; pick-tester / CEng may reject for variance
options_for_user.wall_time_to_v0: ~12 hours (batter backfill 6h + feature build 4h + train 30min + audit/report 1h)

options_for_user.option: B: Re-pull historical odds with intra-day timestamps to recover T-60 strict-pin coverage
actions: Modify backfill script to use multiple snapshot timestamps per game-date (e.g., game_time_utc - 75min as the snapshot param)
Re-pull 2022-09 through 2024 (~3,500 game-dates x 1 snap x 10 credits/call = ~35K credits, ~1% of 5M monthly tier)
Verify timestamp now reflects actual snapshot wall-clock per Odds API response field
Then proceed with full ~5,000-row train set
options_for_user.risk: Odds API historical endpoint accepts any timestamp but returns the nearest archived snap; some games may not have a snap exactly at T-75min; coverage uncertain
options_for_user.wall_time_to_v0: ~24 hours (re-pull design + execution 12h + batter backfill 6h + feature build 4h + train/audit 2h)

options_for_user.option: C: Defer v0 to a smaller season-targeted re-pull
actions: Re-pull only 2024 with corrected per-game snap timestamps (1 month-equivalent of credit, ~5K credits)
Use 2024-Apr to 2024-pre-ASB as train (~1,500 games), 2024-post-ASB as holdout (~400 games)
options_for_user.risk: ~1,500 train rows is even smaller than option A; bootstrap CIs may be wider; one-season training may not capture year-over-year variance

orchestrator_recommendation: Option A. The audit memo's coordination note explicitly anticipated this proxy case. The sample-size shortfall is real but bootstrap CIs + sub-300 ship rule already exist to handle variance. Re-pulling odds (option B) costs another day of wall-time and offers uncertain coverage improvement (the API itself only stores snaps at certain intervals). Option C trades sample size for cleanliness with no clear benefit. Option A respects the spec's documented contingency, runs the missing batter backfill, and lets the empirical gates (CEng's bar) decide whether v0 ships. If it fails the bar, escalate to LightGBM fallback per the proposal's approach_b_fallback path.
